metadata.title: Pypore3D Function Reference for PyQt6 UI
metadata.description: Comprehensive function catalog for porous material analysis software
metadata.target_audience: Scientists analyzing porous materials (biologists, geologists, pharmacists, chemists)
metadata.notes: All functions operate on UNSIGNED image data only. Signed format is not supported.

ui_parsing_guide.overview: This JSON provides all necessary information to dynamically generate UI forms for each pypore3d function. Each function entry contains metadata for sorting/filtering, parameter definitions for form generation, and validation rules.

sorting_fields.module: Filter by source Python module (p3dFiltPy, p3dFiltPy_16, p3dBlobPy, p3dSkelPy, p3dSITKPy, p3dSITKPy_16)
sorting_fields.category: Filter by functional category (Input/Output, Noise Reduction, Segmentation, Morphological Operations, Distance Transform, Connected Components, Quantitative Analysis, Skeletonization, Advanced Filters)
sorting_fields.bit_depth: Filter by image bit depth (8-bit, 16-bit, N/A)

parameter_handling.required_true: Parameter must be provided by user - display as mandatory field
parameter_handling.required_false: Parameter is optional - pre-fill with default value, allow user modification
parameter_handling.is_constant_true: Parameter is locked due to code limitations - display as read-only with constant value
parameter_handling.is_constant_false: Parameter can be modified by user within specified range
parameter_handling.path_info_present: Parameter is a file/folder path - use appropriate file dialog (input=open, output=save)
parameter_handling.range_array: For numeric types, validate input is within [min, max] inclusive
parameter_handling.range_enum: For enumerated types, validate input is one of the allowed values

form_generation_steps: a. Create input field based on 'datatype'
c. If 'is_constant'=true, make field read-only with 'constant_value'
e. Add 'tooltip' as hover help text
f. If 'path_info' exists, attach file/folder browser button
g. Apply validation based on 'range' or 'allowed_values'
5. On form submit, call function using 'function_call' with parameters in 'parameters' order

datatype_mappings.int: Integer spinner or text field with integer validation
datatype_mappings.float: Decimal spinner or text field with float validation
datatype_mappings.bytearray: Memory buffer - typically from previous function output or file load
datatype_mappings.struct: Complex return type - display as expandable result panel

functions.description: Reads a RAW image file in 8-bit unsigned format from disk into memory. The function loads volumetric data stored in raw binary format without headers. For 2D images, set dimz to 0 or 1. This is typically the first step in any image processing workflow.

return_value.description: A matrix of type BYTE (unsigned 8-bit) with dimensions dimx Ã— dimy Ã— dimz representing the loaded image

parameters.description: Full path to the RAW image file to read. The file must exist and be in unsigned 8-bit format.
parameters.tooltip: Select the RAW image file to load. Must be 8-bit unsigned format.

parameters.description: The width of the image in pixels/voxels. Must match the actual image dimensions.
parameters.tooltip: Image width in pixels. Must be greater than 0.

parameters.description: The height of the image in pixels/voxels. Must match the actual image dimensions.
parameters.tooltip: Image height in pixels. Must be greater than 0.

parameters.description: The depth of the image in slices. Set to 0 or 1 for 2D images. For 3D volumes, specify the number of slices.
parameters.tooltip: Number of slices for 3D volumes. Use 0 or 1 for 2D images.

functions.description: Writes an 8-bit unsigned image from memory to a RAW file on disk. The output file contains only raw binary data without headers. This function is used to save processed images for later use or analysis in other software.

return_value.description: Returns the same image data that was written (pass-through)

parameters.description: The 8-bit image data in memory to write to disk.
parameters.tooltip: Image data from a previous operation or loaded image.

parameters.description: Full path where the RAW image file will be saved.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the location and filename for the output RAW file.

parameters.description: The width of the image in pixels/voxels.
parameters.tooltip: Image width in pixels. Must be greater than 0.

parameters.description: The height of the image in pixels/voxels.
parameters.tooltip: Image height in pixels. Must be greater than 0.

parameters.description: The depth of the image in slices. Set to 0 or 1 for 2D images.
parameters.tooltip: Number of slices for 3D volumes. Use 0 or 1 for 2D images.

functions.description: Applies Gaussian smoothing to reduce noise while preserving edges better than mean filtering. The filter uses a bell-shaped kernel that weights central pixels more heavily than neighbors. Effective for removing Gaussian noise while maintaining image structure.

return_value.description: A smoothed image with same dimensions and type as input

parameters.tooltip: Input image data to be smoothed.

parameters.description: The width of the image in pixels/voxels.
parameters.tooltip: Image width. Must be greater than 0.

parameters.description: The height of the image in pixels/voxels.
parameters.tooltip: Image height. Must be greater than 0.

parameters.description: The depth for 3D volumes. Use 0 for 2D images.
parameters.tooltip: Number of slices. Use 0 for 2D images.

parameters.description: The side length of the cubic/square kernel. Due to code limitations, this value is fixed at 3.
parameters.tooltip: Kernel size (fixed at 3 due to implementation).

parameters.description: Standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel. Due to code limitations, this value is fixed at 1.0.
parameters.tooltip: Gaussian sigma (fixed at 1.0 due to implementation).

functions.description: Applies mean (average) filtering for basic noise reduction. Replaces each pixel with the average value of its neighborhood. Simple and fast, but can blur edges. Multiple passes with small kernels can approximate larger kernel effects.

return_value.description: A smoothed image with same dimensions and type as input

parameters.tooltip: Input image data to be smoothed.

parameters.description: The width of the image in pixels/voxels.
parameters.tooltip: Image width. Must be greater than 0.

parameters.description: The height of the image in pixels/voxels.
parameters.tooltip: Image height. Must be greater than 0.

parameters.description: The depth for 3D volumes. Use 0 for 2D images.
parameters.tooltip: Number of slices. Use 0 for 2D images.

parameters.description: Side length of the square/cubic kernel. Must be an odd number. Larger values produce stronger smoothing.
parameters.tooltip: Kernel size (odd number, 3-51). Larger = more smoothing.

functions.description: Applies median filtering for noise reduction while preserving edges. Replaces each pixel with the median value of its neighborhood. Excellent for removing salt-and-pepper noise without blurring edges. Does not create artificial pixel values.

return_value.description: A filtered image with same dimensions and type as input

parameters.tooltip: Input image data to be filtered.

parameters.description: The width of the image in pixels/voxels.
parameters.tooltip: Image width. Must be greater than 0.

parameters.description: The height of the image in pixels/voxels.
parameters.tooltip: Image height. Must be greater than 0.

parameters.description: The depth for 3D volumes. Use 0 for 2D images.
parameters.tooltip: Number of slices. Use 0 for 2D images.

parameters.description: Side length of the square/cubic kernel. Must be an odd number.

functions.signature: (image_data, dimx, dimy, dimz=0, size=3, sigma_d=1.0, sigma_r=3, iterations=10)
functions.description: Edge-preserving smoothing that combines spatial and intensity proximity. Smooths similar-intensity regions while preserving edges by weighting pixels based on both distance and intensity similarity. Excellent for denoising while maintaining structural boundaries.

return_value.description: A filtered image with same dimensions and type as input

parameters.description: The width of the image in pixels/voxels.
The height of the image in pixels/voxels.
The depth for 3D volumes. Use 0 for 2D images.
Side length of the cubic kernel. Must be an odd number.
Standard deviation for spatial (domain) Gaussian. Controls how distance affects weighting.
parameters.tooltip: Spatial sigma. Higher = more distant pixels included.

parameters.description: Standard deviation for range (intensity) Gaussian. Controls how intensity difference affects weighting.
parameters.tooltip: Range sigma. Higher = more intensity variation tolerated.

parameters.description: Number of times to apply the filter. Multiple iterations increase smoothing effect.

functions.signature: (image_data, dimx, dimy, dimz=0, m=1, lambdaP=0.01, sigma=0.01, iterP=10)
functions.description: Edge-enhancing smoothing using anisotropic diffusion. Smooths homogeneous regions while enhancing edges. With small diffusion parameters and long diffusion time, preserves fine structural details better than bilateral filtering. Memory intensive operation.

return_value.description: A filtered image with same dimensions and type as input

parameters.description: The depth for 3D volumes. Use 0 for 2D.
Diffusion mode parameter controlling the smoothing behavior.
Diffusion rate parameter. Higher values = faster diffusion, more smoothing of details.
parameters.tooltip: Diffusion rate. Keep small (0.01-0.1) for detail preservation.

parameters.description: Controls edge sensitivity. Higher values smooth more aggressively across edges.
parameters.tooltip: Edge sensitivity. Keep small to preserve edges.

parameters.description: Number of diffusion iterations. More iterations = longer diffusion time = more smoothing.

functions.signature: (image_data, dimx, dimy, centerX=0, centerY=0, winsize=0, iterations=0, precision=0)
functions.description: Removes ring artifacts from CT reconstructed images using the Boin-Haibel algorithm. Ring artifacts appear as concentric circles in reconstructed tomographic images due to detector defects. This is a 2D-only operation.

return_value.description: A 2D filtered image with ring artifacts reduced

parameters.description: The 2D 8-bit input image with ring artifacts.
X coordinate of ring artifact center. If 0, image center is used.
parameters.tooltip: Ring center X. Use 0 for image center.

parameters.description: Y coordinate of ring artifact center. If 0, image center is used.
parameters.tooltip: Ring center Y. Use 0 for image center.

functions.signature: (image_data, dimx, dimy, centerX=0, centerY=0, winsize=5, thresh=1, iterations=1, precision=1.5, mask=None)
functions.description: Advanced ring artifact removal using the Sijbers-Postnov algorithm. Transforms image to polar coordinates where rings become vertical lines, detects and corrects artifacts, then transforms back. More effective than Boin-Haibel for severe artifacts. 2D only.

return_value.description: A 2D filtered image with ring artifacts removed

parameters.description: The 2D 8-bit input image with ring artifacts.
X coordinate of ring artifact center. If 0, image center is used.
parameters.tooltip: Ring center X. Use 0 for automatic.

parameters.description: Y coordinate of ring artifact center. If 0, image center is used.
parameters.tooltip: Ring center Y. Use 0 for automatic.

parameters.description: Sliding window size for detecting homogeneous rows. Must be odd. Larger windows detect more artifacts but may over-correct.
Homogeneity threshold. ~3Ã— image noise variance is recommended. Lower = more sensitive to faint artifacts.
Number of correction passes. More passes for severe artifacts.
Polar-cartesian conversion precision. Higher = better quality but slower.
Optional 2D mask (255=process, 0=ignore). Limits processing to region of interest for faster execution.
parameters.tooltip: Optional ROI mask. None = process entire image.

functions.description: Removes connected components (blobs) that touch image borders. Useful for eliminating incomplete objects at volume edges that would skew analysis. Uses connected component labeling to identify and remove border-touching regions.

return_value.description: Binary image with border-connected objects removed

parameters.description: The depth for 3D volumes. Use 0 for 2D.
Voxel connectivity for component detection. 6=faces only, 18=faces+edges, 26=faces+edges+corners.

functions.description: Segments grayscale image into binary using automatically computed threshold. Seven methods available: (1) Kittler-Illingworth, (2) Otsu, (3) Pun, (4) Ridler-Calvard, (5) Kapur, (6) Tsai moment-preserving, (7) Huang-Yager. Each method optimal for different image types.

parameters.description: Output parameter receiving the computed threshold value (0-255). This is computed by the function.

functions.description: Reads a RAW image file in 16-bit format from disk. Supports both little-endian (default) and big-endian byte orders. The library internally uses unsigned format; signed images should be converted externally.

return_value.description: A matrix of type UINT16 with dimensions dimx Ã— dimy Ã— dimz

parameters.description: Full path to the 16-bit RAW image file.
The depth for 3D volumes. Use 0 for 2D.
Data signedness: 0=unsigned (default), 1=signed. Note: Library uses unsigned internally.

functions.signature: (image_data, filename, dimx, dimy, dimz=0, is_little=1, is_signed=0)
functions.description: Writes a 16-bit image from memory to a RAW file. Supports both endianness options and signed/unsigned format specification.

parameters.description: Full path for the output file.

functions.description: Applies Gaussian smoothing to 16-bit images. Same algorithm as 8-bit version but preserves 16-bit dynamic range. Note: Kernel size and sigma are fixed due to implementation.

parameters.description: Kernel size (fixed at 3 due to implementation).
Gaussian sigma (fixed at 1.0 due to implementation).

functions.description: Applies mean filtering to 16-bit images. Replaces each voxel with the average of its neighborhood. Preserves 16-bit dynamic range.

parameters.description: Kernel side length. Must be odd.

functions.description: Applies median filtering to 16-bit images. Excellent for salt-and-pepper noise removal while preserving edges and 16-bit dynamic range.

parameters.description: Kernel side length. Must be odd.

functions.signature: (image_data, dimx, dimy, dimz=0, size=3, sigma_d=1.0, sigma_r=3, iterations=10)
functions.description: Edge-preserving smoothing for 16-bit images using bilateral filtering. Preserves full 16-bit dynamic range while reducing noise.

parameters.description: Kernel side length. Must be odd.

functions.signature: (image_data, dimx, dimy, dimz=0, m=1, lambdaP=0.01, sigma=0.01, iterP=10)
functions.description: Edge-enhancing anisotropic diffusion for 16-bit images. Preserves fine details while smoothing homogeneous regions. Memory intensive.

functions.signature: (image_data, dimx, dimy, centerX=0, centerY=0, winsize=5, thresh=1, iterations=1, precision=1.5, is_bit12=False, mask=None)
functions.description: Ring artifact removal for 16-bit images using Sijbers-Postnov algorithm. Includes option for 12-bit data stored in 16-bit format. 2D only.

parameters.description: Enable for 12-bit data in 16-bit container. Adjusts threshold normalization.
parameters.tooltip: Check if data is 12-bit in 16-bit format.

functions.description: Automatic thresholding for 16-bit images. Same seven methods as 8-bit version. Output threshold is in range 0-65535.
Converts a 16-bit image to 8-bit by rescaling intensity values. Maps the full 16-bit range (0-65535) to 8-bit range (0-255).

Computes fundamental 3D morphometric parameters for porous materials: volume fraction (porosity), specific surface area, mean curvature integral, and Euler characteristic (connectivity). Essential first-step analysis for any porous sample.

fields.name: Volume Fraction (Vv)
fields.description: Ratio of object voxels to total voxels (porosity/density)

fields.name: Specific Surface Area (Sv)
fields.description: Surface area per unit volume, related to mechanical properties

fields.name: Euler Characteristic (Cv)
fields.description: Connectivity index of the object network

parameters.description: Physical size of one voxel in mm. Use 1.0 for results in voxel units.
parameters.tooltip: Voxel size in mm (e.g., 0.001 for 1 micron).

functions.description: Analyzes directional dependence of pore structure. Computes isotropy index (uniformity of distribution) and elongation index (preferred orientation). Essential for materials where directional properties matter (e.g., trabecular bone, fibrous materials).
Identifies and labels separate connected regions (blobs) in a binary image. Each blob receives a unique gray level. Maximum 65535 blobs supported. Essential preprocessing for blob analysis.

return_value.description: 16-bit labeled image where each blob has unique gray value

functions.description: Extracts only the largest connected component from a binary image. Useful for isolating the main structure and removing small noise particles or satellite objects.

return_value.description: Binary image containing only the largest blob

functions.description: Extracts only the smallest connected component from a binary image. Can be useful for identifying minimum feature sizes or isolated particles.

return_value.description: Binary image containing only the smallest blob

functions.description: Removes connected components smaller than a specified volume threshold. Effective for eliminating noise particles while preserving meaningful structures. Input must be binary (255=object).

return_value.description: Filtered binary image with small blobs removed

parameters.description: Blobs with fewer voxels than this threshold are removed.
parameters.tooltip: Minimum blob size to keep (in voxels).

functions.signature: (image_data, dimx, dimy, dimz=0, w1=3, w2=4, w3=5)
functions.description: Computes distance transform using chamfer (weighted) distances. Each object voxel receives a value representing its distance to the nearest background. Common weight sets: Cityblock <1,-,->, Chessboard <1,1,1>, Quasi-Euclidean <3,4,5>.

Computes true Euclidean distance transform with squared values (to avoid floating point). Each object voxel receives the squared distance to the nearest background voxel. Take square root for actual distances.

Computes trabecular bone-style morphometric parameters: BV/TV (bone volume fraction), Tb.Th (trabecular thickness), Tb.Sp (trabecular separation), Tb.N (trabecular number). Applicable to any trabecular-like porous structure.

fields.name: Trabecular Number (Tb.N)
fields.description: Number of structure traversals per unit length

parameters.description: Depth for 3D volumes (required for this analysis).

functions.description: Computes fractal dimension of the structure, a texture measure indicating structural complexity and self-similarity across scales. Values typically range from 2 (smooth surface) to 3 (space-filling).

functions.signature: (image_data, dimx, dimy, dimz=0, blob_im=None, star_im=None, resolution=1.0, conn=6, blob_analysis_file='blob_analysis.txt')
functions.description: Comprehensive analysis of isolated pores/particles. Computes for each blob: volume, max inscribed sphere, equivalent sphere, min/max axis lengths, sphericity, aspect ratio, and extent. Results saved to text file.

fields.name: Max Inscribed Sphere Diameter
fields.description: Diameter of largest sphere fitting inside each blob

fields.name: Equivalent Sphere Diameter
fields.description: Diameter of sphere with same volume

fields.name: Minor Axis Length
fields.description: Shortest axis through center of mass

fields.name: Major Axis Length
fields.description: Longest axis through center of mass

parameters.tooltip: Binary image with blobs to analyze.

parameters.description: Optional output: image with maximal inscribed spheres.
Optional output: image showing min/max axes as 'stars'.

functions.description: Labels skeleton voxels by topological type: nodes (junctions), end points, isolated points, and branch types. Essential for understanding skeleton topology.

functions.signature: (image_data, dimx, dimy, dimz=0, mu=0.15, eps=1E-4, hierarc=1.0, scale=0.0)
functions.description: Computes centerline skeleton using Gradient Vector Flow method. Produces smooth, well-centered skeletons. Computationally intensive but high quality.

parameters.description: Controls GVF field smoothness. Higher = smoother skeleton.
Controls node-to-end branches. Higher = more branches.

functions.description: Computes skeleton using PalÃ¡gyi-Kuba parallel thinning. Fast and topology-preserving. Good for connectivity analysis.

functions.signature: (image_data, dimx, dimy, dimz=0, thresh=3, ultimate=False, iterative=False)
functions.description: Removes spurious branches from skeleton. Simple mode removes short branches. Ultimate removes all node-to-end branches.

parameters.description: Maximum length of branches to remove.

functions.signature: (image_data, skeleton_image, dimx, dimy, dimz=0, nodes_im=None, pores_im=None, ends_im=None, throats_im=None, merging_factor=0.85, tortuosity_depth=3, resolution=1.0, skel_stats_file='skeleton_stats.txt')
functions.description: Comprehensive pore network analysis. Computes connectivity density, pore sizes, throat sizes, branch lengths. Results saved to file.
functions.example_usage: stats = py_p3dSkeletonAnalysis(binary, skeleton, 512, 512, 100, None, None, None, None, 0.85, 3, 0.001, 'results.txt')

parameters.description: Optional: output for end point visualization.

functions.description: Morphological dilation expands foreground regions. Object boundaries grow outward.
Morphological erosion shrinks foreground regions. Object boundaries recede inward.
Suppresses minima shallower than specified depth. Used before watershed segmentation.

return_value.description: Filtered image with shallow minima suppressed

parameters.description: Minimum depth for minima to survive.

functions.description: Segments image into multiple phases using Otsu multi-thresholding.

parameters.description: Number of phases to segment into.

functions.description: Computes edge strength at each pixel. High values indicate strong edges.
Edge-preserving smoothing using level-set curvature flow.
Classic anisotropic diffusion using gradient-based conductance.
Modified curvature diffusion equation. Less sensitive to contrast than gradient-based.
Variant of curvature flow with adaptive diffusion. Produces sharper edges.

functions.signature: (input_image, dimx, dimy, dimz=0, level=0, markWatershedLine=False, connected=False)
functions.description: Morphological watershed segmentation. Use with gradient or distance transform.

parameters.description: Input image (typically distance transform or gradient).

functions.description: Unsupervised multi-phase segmentation using K-means clustering.
16-bit H-minima transform using file-based I/O. For large 16-bit datasets.

return_value.description: SimpleITK image object (also written to .mhd file)

functions.signature: (dist_file_path, watershed_image_path, dimx, dimy, dimz=0, level=0, markWatershedLine=True, connected=False)
functions.description: 16-bit watershed segmentation using file-based I/O. For large 16-bit distance maps.

return_value.description: SimpleITK image object (also written to .mhd file)
